name: Hunter Henry
headline: TE7 finish hides a target share about to shrink
body:
Henry's 2025 was his strongest New England campaign: a TE7 finish on 118.8 fantasy points, 60 grabs for 768 yards and 7 scores across all 17 games. The efficiency was real, with 0.433 EPA per play, a 63.2% success rate and 1.65 yards per route run, all season bests. The volume that fueled it stands out most: an 18% target share, 22 red-zone looks and 10 end-zone targets, the kind of scoring opportunity that drives touchdown totals.

That usage is the catch. His seven touchdowns came on a near-decade-high 8.8 yards per target, a spike that rarely repeats, and the depth chart got crowded. New arrivals at receiver thin the pecking order around Drake Maye, threatening the red-zone share that made him valuable. Drafted as a back-end TE1 near pick 149, he's a steady floor with realistic regression baked in. Projections of roughly 57-665-6 reflect a likely step back from his TE7 ceiling.
